title: String functions
summary: String functions in Calculated Columns and SQL Expressions

sketchBlocks.title: What are SQL string functions?
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
SQL string functions let you manipulate text data in your queries. For example, you can change text to uppercase, extract part of a string, or combine columns. These functions are useful for cleaning up or formatting output in reports.

Common string functions in SQLite include:
- `UPPER(text)`: Converts text to uppercase.
- `LOWER(text)`: Converts text to lowercase.
- `LENGTH(text)`: Returns the number of characters in the text.
- `SUBSTR(text, start, length)`: Extracts a substring from the text.
- `TRIM(text)`: Removes spaces from the start and end of the text.

Example:
```sql
SELECT customer_name, UPPER(region) AS region_upper FROM orders;
```
This query shows each customer's name and their region in uppercase.

sketchBlocks.title: Using string functions in calculated columns
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
You can use string functions in calculated columns to create new values or clean up data. For example, if you want to show only the first three letters of each category, you can use `SUBSTR`:

```sql
SELECT category, SUBSTR(category, 1, 3) AS cat_short FROM orders;
```

This query adds a new column called `cat_short` with the first three letters of the category.

quizDraft.title: Show customer names in uppercase
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to select the `customer_name` column from the `orders` table, but display all names in uppercase. Name the output column `customer_upper`.
quizDraft.hint: Use the UPPER() function and an alias.

help.concept: The UPPER() function converts text to uppercase. Use SELECT with an alias to rename the output column.
help.hint_1: Apply UPPER() to the customer_name column and use AS to rename it.
help.hint_2: Your SELECT should include UPPER(customer_name) AS customer_upper.

quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT UPPER(customer_name) AS customer_upper FROM orders;

quizDraft.title: Extract region initials
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to select the first two letters of the `region` column from the `orders` table. Name the output column `region_initials`.
quizDraft.hint: Use the SUBSTR() function to extract part of the string.

help.concept: The SUBSTR() function extracts a substring from a text value. It takes the column, a starting position, and a length.
help.hint_1: Start at position 1 and extract 2 characters from region.
help.hint_2: Use SUBSTR(region, 1, 2) and give it an alias.

quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT SUBSTR(region, 1, 2) AS region_initials FROM orders;

quizDraft.title: Which function returns the number of characters in a string?
quizDraft.prompt: Which SQL function returns the number of characters in a text value?
quizDraft.hint: Think about which function measures string length.

help.concept: The LENGTH() function returns the number of characters in a string.
help.hint_2: Look for a function name that means 'size' or 'length'.

quizDraft.title: Select all true statements about string functions
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following statements about SQL string functions are true? Select all that apply.

help.concept: String functions can change, extract, or measure text values in SQL.
help.hint_1: Some functions change case, others extract parts or measure length.
help.hint_2: Think about what UPPER, LOWER, and LENGTH do.

options.content: LENGTH() returns the number of characters in a string.
SUBSTR() removes spaces from a string.

quizDraft.title: Fill in the blank: Removing spaces
quizDraft.prompt: Which function removes spaces from the beginning and end of a string?

help.concept: The TRIM() function removes leading and trailing spaces from text.
help.hint_1: This function is used for cleaning up whitespace.
help.hint_2: It starts with the letter T.

quizDraft.template: The function that removes spaces from the start and end of a string is _____.
